result:
Done. I implemented the three missing operations in `CsvRow.cs`, and the project builds cleanly with zero warnings.

- `EscapeField` returns the field verbatim unless `NeedsQuoting` holds, in which case it doubles internal quotes and wraps the result in double-quotes.
- `JoinRow` escapes each field and joins with commas (`string.Join` over `Select(EscapeField)`), so an empty list yields the empty string.
- `SplitRow` is a single-pass parser: a field starting with `"` is consumed until the closing un-doubled quote (with `""` contributing one literal quote, and commas/CR/LF inside treated as content); otherwise the field runs to the next comma or end of line. The loop structure makes the empty line parse to one empty field and a trailing comma produce a trailing empty field.

I verified the logic with a scratch script (mirroring the exact code, run outside the project and deleted afterward): round-trips of `SplitRow(JoinRow(fields))` pass for edge cases including embedded commas, quotes, CR/LF, empty fields at every position, and the empty-line/empty-list special cases. `NeedsQuoting` was left untouched, and everything is pure computation as required.
